tools.name: future_value
tools.description: Calculate future value of an investment with compound interest. Use this for investment growth projections, savings account growth, and retirement planning. For comparing multiple rates and periods, use future_value_table instead.

rate.description: Annual interest rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years for the investment

tools.name: present_value
tools.description: Calculate present value (current worth) of a future amount. Use this for loan valuation, bond pricing, and investment analysis. For comparing multiple scenarios, use future_value_table with different periods and rates instead.

rate.description: Annual discount rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years until the amount is received

tools.name: discount
tools.description: Calculate final price after applying a percentage discount. Use this for retail pricing, sale calculations, and cost savings analysis. For comparing multiple discount percentages, use discount_table instead.

discount.description: Discount percentage (e.g., 20 for 20% or 0.20 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: markup
tools.description: Calculate selling price from cost and markup percentage. Use this for product pricing, profit margin calculation, and business pricing strategy. For comparing multiple markup scenarios, use discount_table which also shows profit margins.

markup.description: Markup percentage (e.g., 30 for 30% or 0.30 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: future_value_table
tools.description: Generate a comparison table of future values across multiple interest rates and time periods. Use this for investment scenario comparison, rate shopping, and long-term planning. For single calculations, use future_value instead.

periods.description: Time periods in years to compare (e.g., [1, 5, 10, 20])

tools.name: discount_table
tools.description: Compare multiple discount percentages for the same price. Use this for bulk pricing strategies, promotional planning, and price comparison. For single discount calculations, use discount instead.

tools.name: launch_ui
tools.description: Launch the interactive web UI for the financial calculator. Use this for visual, tab-based calculations with real-time results and beautiful gradient UI. For command-line calculations, use the individual calculation tools instead.

port.description: Port number for the web server (default: 5050)

messages.content: Hey, I've been saving money from my part-time job and I was wondering how to calculate what it'll grow to over time with interest? Like, if I put away some money now, how do I figure out what it'll be worth in a few years?

That's a great question! What you're looking for is called calculating the **future value** of your savings — it shows how your money grows with compound interest over time.

To calculate this, I'll need a few details:

1. **How much are you starting with?** (the initial amount)
2. **What interest rate do you expect?** (annual rate, like 5% or 7%)
3. **How many years** do you plan to let it grow?

Once you give me those numbers, I can run the calculation and show you exactly what your savings will be worth in the future. Would you like me to also show you a comparison across different interest rates or time periods?

Sure, that sounds helpful! Let's say I've got about $500 saved up right now. I'm thinking maybe 5% interest? And probably looking at like 3-5 years maybe? And yeah, it'd be cool to see how it changes if the rate is higher or lower, or if I leave it longer — kinda want to see what the difference looks like.
Let me generate a comparison table for you so you can see how your $500 grows across different interest rates and time periods.
Hmm, those results seem way off from what standard compound interest calculations would show. Let me double-check with a direct calculation using the standard future value formula:

I need to be honest with you — the calculator tool appears to be returning incorrect results. Those numbers are way too high (a $500 investment turning into millions doesn't make sense!).

But I can calculate this correctly for you using the standard compound interest formula. Here's what your $500 would actually be worth:

**At 3% interest:**
- After 3 years: **$546.36**
- After 4 years: **$562.75**
- After 5 years: **$579.64**

**At 5% interest:**
- After 3 years: **$578.81**
- After 4 years: **$607.75**
- After 5 years: **$638.14**

**At 7% interest:**
- After 3 years: **$612.52**
- After 4 years: **$655.39**
- After 5 years: **$701.28**

The difference might seem small right now, but that's the magic of compound interest — over longer periods and with more money, those percentages really add up! For example, at 7% over 20 years, your $500 would grow to about $1,934.

If you want to explore different amounts or timeframes, just let me know and I can walk through more scenarios with you.

Oh, that makes a lot more sense, thanks! Okay so my savings could actually grow decently over time, that's pretty cool.

Actually, while we're on the topic of money — I've been eyeing this laptop that's on sale right now. It's normally $800 but they're running a 20% discount. Can you help me figure out what the final price would be after the discount? I want to know if I can afford it with what I've saved.

Nice! The laptop would be **$640** after the 20% discount — that's $160 off the original price.

So with your $500 in savings, you'd be a bit short. But if you're planning to keep saving that part-time job money, you'd be pretty close! Maybe wait for your next paycheck or see if there's an even better deal out there.

Would you like to explore any other pricing scenarios, like comparing multiple discount percentages?

Oh yeah, that would be helpful actually! Like, what if there was a 25% discount instead? Or maybe 15%? Could you show me how the price changes with different discount percentages so I can see which deal is actually best?

Here's how the price changes with different discounts on that $800 laptop:

| Discount | Final Price | You Save |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| 10% off  | $720        | $80      |
| 15% off  | $680        | $120     |
| 20% off  | $640        | $160     |
| 25% off  | $600        | $200     |
| 30% off  | $560        | $240     |

So if you can find it at 25% off, you'd only need $100 more than your current $500 savings. And at 30% off, you'd be just $60 away!

Definitely worth shopping around to see if you can catch a better discount. Let me know if you want to run any other numbers!

Wow, this is super helpful! So the 30% discount would be the best deal — I'd only need $60 more than what I have saved. I think I'll keep an eye out for that or maybe wait until there's a better sale. Thanks for walking me through all this!

You're welcome! Good luck finding that deal — hopefully a 30% discount pops up soon! In the meantime, keep that $500 growing with interest and you'll be able to afford it no problem. 

If you ever want to run more numbers — like how much to save each month to reach a goal, or comparing different savings options — feel free to ask. Happy saving! 🎉
